patch_description:
Current feature extraction uses a single center-cropped 60-frame window, discarding most temporal information in longer sequences (up to 3015 frames). This is highly suboptimal for gait analysis where rhythmic patterns and asymmetries are distributed across time.

I propose to replace the single crop with multi-window attention pooling:
- Slide a 60-frame window across each sequence with 50% overlap
- Extract MotionCLIP embedding for each window
- Apply learnable attention pooling over all window embeddings to produce a single sequence-level representation

This preserves temporal structure, captures multiple gait cycles, and allows the model to focus on most discriminative phases (e.g., stance vs swing). Attention pooling is differentiable and handles variable numbers of windows naturally.

The change requires modifying extract_features() to implement sliding windows and attention pooling, and updating ClassifierMLP to handle the same input dimension (still 512-dim).
